mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, invalidating the labor deterioration channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction sector activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon through improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in >55% of cases, breaking the demand synchronization link.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 momentum as supply-side constraints ease and inventory turnover improves.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of >1% fail to predict positive 5d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above the 230 index threshold triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise and real yields increase.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 230.0 coincides with positive 20d returns in >65% of observations, negating the inflation-fear transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive quarterly S&P 500 returns through higher household income and consumption.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth > 150k combined with UNRATE decline fails to yield positive 60d returns in >50% of cases, indicating a broken wage-consumption channel.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger corporate revenue fundamentals and economic resilience, driving positive short-term S&P 500 price momentum as equity risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% is followed by negative 5d returns in >60% of instances, disproving the short-term GDP momentum effect.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production alongside falling personal consumption expenditures signal a synchronized supply-demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns during recessionary transitions as earnings forecasts are slashed.
failure_condition: Synchronized declines in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to predict negative 20d returns with a Rank IC > -0.05, invalidating the contraction signal.
